nav_logout_confirm.title: Sign out
nav_logout_confirm.message: You will need to log in again to access the app.

rail_toggle.title: Expandable sidebar
rail_toggle.content: Click this button to expand the sidebar and see section names. Your preference is saved automatically.

rail_overview.title: Side menu
rail_overview.content: The menu is organized in groups: main actions at the top, then Observability, Configuration, and Admin below.

rail_workflows.title: Workflows
rail_workflows.content: Create and manage your automation flows with AI agents.

rail_agents.title: Agents
rail_agents.content: Configure agent profiles that will be used in your workflows.

rail_events.title: Executions
rail_events.content: Track the execution history of your workflows in real time.

rail_rag.title: RAG Bases
rail_rag.content: Manage the knowledge bases that power your agents.

cta.title: Ready to start?
cta.content: Click Workflows then New agent to create your first AI agent. The builder tour will guide you step by step!

stats.title: Metrics panel
stats.content: Here you see a real-time summary: how many bases exist, total documents, indexed chunks, and how many queries and ingestions happened in the last 24 hours.

create_btn.title: Create a knowledge base
create_btn.content: Click here to create a new Knowledge Base. Give it a name, a unique identifier, and a description — the base will be ready to receive documents.

kb_list.title: Your knowledge bases
kb_list.content: Each card represents a base. Click 'Open base documents' to see indexed documents, the semantic coverage score, and add new files.

docs_section.title: Documents and coverage
docs_section.content: When you open a base you see all ingested documents, the indexing status of each one (pending → indexed), and you can add new files by clicking 'Add Document'.

coverage.title: Semantic Coverage
coverage.content: This indicator shows how well the documents cover the semantic space of the base. The higher it is, the more varied and complete the contents — this improves the quality of agent responses.

add_doc.title: Ingest documents
add_doc.content: Add PDFs, DOCX, TXT, Markdown, CSV, JSON and other formats (up to 25 MB). Archon splits the file into chunks, generates embeddings, and indexes them vectorially — in seconds the agent can already query the content.

overview.title: Welcome to the Builder
overview.content: Here you build AI agents visually — no code needed. Drag nodes, connect them, and configure agent behavior in minutes.

palette.title: Node palette
palette.content: All available node types are here: Planner (LLM reasoning), HTTP, RAG, Transformers and more. Drag any item onto the canvas.

planner.title: Start with the Planner
planner.content: The Planner is the heart of every agent. It receives the user message, reasons via LLM, and decides which action to take. Drag it to the canvas!

canvas.title: Build your flow here
canvas.content: Drop the Planner on the canvas and add more nodes. Connect them by dragging from the output port (right) of one node to the input port (left) of another.

inspector.title: Configure each node
inspector.content: Click any node to open the Inspector. Here you set the LLM provider, model, system instructions and all agent parameters.

nameInput.title: Name your agent
nameInput.content: Give the agent a descriptive name. The ID below is auto-generated and identifies the agent in the API — you can edit it before saving.

save.title: Save to backend
save.content: Click Save to persist the agent. It will be available for conversations, tests and API integrations.

simulate.title: Test right away
simulate.content: Use Simulate to send a real message to the agent and watch the execution flow step by step in the events panel.
